channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Expert Calls Kalpakkam Nuclear Reactor Attaining Criticality As ‘Step Towards Atmanirbhar Bharat’
item.description: Nuclear energy expert Dr Neelam Goyal on Tuesday called the attainment of criticality at the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) in Tamil Nadu’s Kalpakkam, as a “big win for India” and an “important step towards ‘Atmanirbhar Bharat’.”
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cross-border drug smuggling a major challenge for border states: CM Omar Abdullah
item.description: Chief Minister Omar Abdullah on Tuesday said drug smuggling from across the border remains a major challenge, not limited to Jammu and Kashmir, but faced by all border states across the country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 2 dead, 20 injured in firing after mob storms CRPF camp in Manipur
item.description: At least two persons were killed and 20 others injured in firing by security forces after hundreds of people stormed a CRPF camp in Manipur’s Bishnupur district and burnt vehicles during a protest on Tuesday, the police said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 15 Gelatin Sticks, Over 50 Detonators: Cache Of Explosives Recovered In Nagpur’s Residential Area
item.description: A large cache of explosives was recovered from a residential locality near CA Road in Nagpur on Tuesday, raising serious security concerns in one of the city’s most sensitive zones, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IndiGo aircraft hit by unmanned vehicle at Kolkata airport, plane grounded
item.description: An unmanned third-party vehicle on the apron hit a parked IndiGo aircraft at Kolkata airport on Tuesday, prompting the airline to ground the plane for inspection, the carrier said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Ghatak UCAV and AMCA to Get Internal Astra Mk2 with Folding Fins
item.description: India’s transition toward stealth-centric air combat is driving a critical evolution in its missile technology, with idrw.org learning from sources that DRDO is actively developing a specialized Astra Mk2 variant featuring folding fins for internal carriage. The project has been designated as a high-priority effort, reflecting its importance in enabling next-generation platforms like the AMCA and the Ghatak UCAV to maintain low radar signatures while carrying potent air-to-air weaponry.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India to Conduct GNSS Jammer Trials in Bay of Bengal: Strengthening Electronic Warfare Edge in Contested Battlespace
item.description: India has issued a fresh notification for GNSS Jammer Trials in the Bay of Bengal region, scheduled for 11-12 April 2026. This latest development signals a determined push toward mastering advanced electronic warfare (EW) capabilities, enabling the country to deny critical satellite-based navigation to potential adversaries during high-intensity conflicts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Challenge to Develop High-Fidelity Digital Twin for Gas Turbine Starter Engine
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has initiated a new innovation push under its DRISHTI challenge framework, inviting the design, development, and creation of a high-fidelity digital twin software model for a gas turbine starter engine. The move reflects HAL’s growing focus on adopting data-driven engineering tools to enhance performance monitoring, predictive maintenance, and lifecycle management of critical aerospace systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan Navy Chief Claims Readiness to Sink INS Vikrant in Operation Sindoor – Satellite Images Reveal Warships Hiding in Commercial Ports
item.description: Pakistan Navy Chief Admiral Naveed Ashraf recently claimed that his forces were fully prepared to sink India’s aircraft carrier INS Vikrant during Operation Sindoor in May 2025. Speaking at the induction ceremony of a new MILGEM-class corvette, Admiral Ashraf asserted that Pakistani naval and air assets had effectively trapped the Indian carrier battle group, forcing it to remain in safer waters while Pakistan maintained a strong deterrent posture.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tejas Mk2 vs Rafale: Can India’s Next-Gen Fighter Outturn a Proven Omnirole Combat Beast?
item.description: The comparison between the HAL Tejas Mk2 and the Dassault Rafale has become one of the most technically intense debates in Indian defence circles as of 2026. At its core, this is not just a comparison of two aircraft—but of two design philosophies: a combat-proven omnirole platform versus a newer-generation design optimized for sensor dominance and agility.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Human Drama Behind Operation Sindoor: S-400 Battery Comes Alive in the Dead of Night
item.description: In the tense hours following India’s decisive strikes during Operation Sindoor, one of the most gripping moments unfolded not in the skies above Pakistan, but on the ground at an Indian air defence site. As Pakistani retaliation unfolded with incoming threats, a young Wing Commander—later identified in accounts as part of Group Captain Animesh Patni’s S-400 regiment—stood ready to unleash one of the world’s most advanced air defence systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Air Marshal Tiwari Rejects ‘Rocket Force’ Concept, Calls for Focus on Modern Fighter Fleet Instead
item.description: Retired Air Marshal Narmdeshwar Tiwari has pushed back against the growing debate around creating a dedicated “rocket force” for India, arguing that such a structure is neither aligned with Indian military doctrine nor proven across most advanced air power-centric nations. Instead, he has emphasized that India’s priority should remain on strengthening its fleet of modern fighter aircraft and precision air-delivered capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Don’t Test India’: Rajnath cites 1971 war after Pakistan’s Kolkata strike threat
item.description: Days after Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khwaja Asif threatened to strike Kolkata if India carried out any “false flag operations”, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh invoked the decisive 1971 war and the birth of Bangladesh to caution Islamabad against escalating tensions. “Pakistan’s Defence Minister should not have given such a provocative statement. 55 years ago, they suffered the consequences when Pakistan was divided into two parts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Gulf war rewrites strategy: Chokepoint, drones and attack subs
item.description: The outcome of the war in West Asia has sent India’s defence planners in a tizzy working out new plans, fast forwarding some as they work out new strategies which include guarding maritime chokepoints and readying drone forces for naval or distant wars in the future. For a country that depends on seaborne routes for nearly 90 per cent of its trade by volume and 80 per cent of its crude oil imports, the protection of chokepoints such as the Strait of Hormuz, Bab el-Mandeb, and Strait of Malacca has moved from a strategic consideration to an existential imperative.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Fast Breeder Reactors To Deliver Reliable, Higher Thermal Efficiency: Govt
item.description: Fast breeder reactors will play a crucial role in delivering reliable, low-carbon base-load power with higher thermal efficiency, the government said on Tuesday, as India’s 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) achieved first criticality.
item.dc:creator: admin
